source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/■ 1차 ADD_510(k)_submission_Final_201023.zip/WORD/VOL_007_Appendix F Safety Test Report/006_Attachment 3 IEC 60601-1-6 Test report (No. 397950_1).pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1-6 test report (report No. 397950_1)
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. 397950_1; TRF No. IEC60601_1_6H; Master TRF dated 2017-08

sourceSummary.oneLine: Nemko Korea IEC 60601-1-6 usability test report for the Abyz Digital Detector model ADD, issued 2020-09-28.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports CB Scheme safety/usability evidence and states it shall be used with IEC 60601-1 main test report No. 397950.
keyPoints: The report covers IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 for medical electrical equipment usability.
The test item is described as Abyz Digital Detector with model/type reference ADD.
The usability engineering process was considered under IEC 62366:2007 + AMD1:2014 and received pass verdicts in the checklist.
The report states the manufacturer established a usability engineering process, acceptance criteria, and demonstrated that acceptance criteria were met.
The report notes no User Interface of Unknown Provenance (UOUP).

concepts.name: IEC 60601-1-6 usability collateral standard
concepts.evidence: Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6 General requirements for safety - Collateral Standard: Usability

concepts.name: IEC 62366 usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: IEC 62366 is considered, see Annex I in this test report.

concepts.name: Usability Engineering File
concepts.evidence: Inspection of the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE verified that the MANUFACTURER established a USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS

concepts.name: Usability Validation Plan
concepts.evidence: USABILITY VALIDATION plan Usability validation plan (A-TCF-5-039, Rev. 0) Established.

concepts.name: Primary Operating Functions
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER determined the PRIMARY OPERATING FUNCTIONS and recorded them in the USABILITY FILE

concepts.name: User Interface of Unknown Provenance
concepts.evidence: USER INTERFACE OF UNKNOWN PROVENANCE (UOUP) was evaluated according to Annex K rather than the requirements of 5.1 through 5.9. No UOUP.

evidence.claim: The document is IEC 60601-1-6 test report No. 397950_1.
Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. prepared the report.
evidence.evidenceText: Name of Testing Laboratory preparing the Report: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The applicant is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The tested item is the Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item description: Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The model/type reference for the tested item is ADD.
The test specification includes IEC 60601-1-6:2010, AMD1:2013 for use with IEC 62366:2007, AMD1:2014 and IEC 60601-1 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Standard: IEC 60601-1-6:2010, AMD1:2013 for use in conjunction with IEC 62366:2007, AMD1:2014 and IEC 60601-1:2005, COR1:2006, COR2:2007, AMD1: 2012 or equivalent consolidated version IEC 60601-1:2012 (Edition 3.1)

evidence.claim: The test procedure is CB Scheme.
The report shall be used with IEC 60601-1 test report No. 397950.
evidence.evidenceText: This test report No. 397950_1 shall be used in conjunction with IEC 60601-1 test report (No. 397950).

evidence.claim: The device is classified for installation and use as portable ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification of installation and use: Portable ME equipment

evidence.claim: The test item was received on 2020-06-12 and tests were performed from 2020-06-23 to 2020-09-25.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of receipt of test item: 2020-06-12; Date(s) of performance of tests: 2020-06-23 to 2020-09-25

evidence.claim: The manufacturer established a usability engineering process.
evidence.evidenceText: Inspection of the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE verified that the MANUFACTURER established a USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS

evidence.claim: The manufacturer established acceptance criteria for usability.
evidence.evidenceText: Inspection of the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE verified that the MANUFACTURER established acceptance criteria for USABILITY

evidence.claim: The manufacturer demonstrated that usability acceptance criteria were met.
evidence.evidenceText: Inspection of the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE verified that the MANUFACTURER demonstrated that the acceptance criteria for USABILITY have been met.

evidence.claim: The instructions for use include a brief description of the ME equipment, operating principles, and significant physical and performance characteristics relevant to usability.
evidence.evidenceText: The instructions for use include a brief description of the ME EQUIPMENT, its physical operating principles and significant physical and performance characteristics relevant to its USABILITY Included in the user manual. P

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process checklist under IEC 62366:2007 + AMD1:2014 is included as Annex I.
evidence.evidenceText: ANNEX I - IEC 62366:2007 + AMD1:2014 – Usability engineering process checklist

evidence.claim: Known or foreseeable hazards related to usability were identified according to ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Identification of known or foreseeable HAZARDS related to USABILITY according to ISO 14971:2007, Cl. 4.3 ... Identified according to ISO 14971. P

evidence.claim: No User Interface of Unknown Provenance was present.
evidence.evidenceText: USER INTERFACE OF UNKNOWN PROVENANCE (UOUP) was evaluated according to Annex K rather than the requirements of 5.1 through 5.9. No UOUP. N/A

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal and sourcePath indicate FDA/510(k), but the provided source text identifies a CB Scheme/IECEE/Nemko test report and does not state FDA authority.
Product signal lists HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the source text only identifies model/type reference ADD.
Trade mark field is blank in the source text.
